title: rubus glabrefactus
intro: Rubus glabrefactus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family native to North America. The medicinal properties of Rubus glabrefactus include its high content of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. Rubus glabrefactus is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, and it may also be used fresh in salads or as a jam ingredient due to its sweet and slightly tart flavor.
